reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the informal claim: given an arbitrary index type ι and a family of topological spaces X : ι → Type*, the hypothesis `∀ i, Nonempty (X i)` matches "each X_α is nonempty", the hypothesis `NormalSpace (Π i, X i)` matches "∏ X_α is normal", and the conclusion `∀ i, NormalSpace (X i)` matches "each X_α is normal". The quantifier structure (universally over all factors), the types, and the use of the product topology `Π i, X i` all align with the informal statement. The notion of normality is used consistently on both sides, so the formalization is neither stronger nor weaker.
